word_list.word: life is not all beer and skittles
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪf ˌɪzənt ˈɔːl bˈɪɹ ænd skˈɪɾəlz

senses.definition: used to suggest that while there may be times of happiness, there will also be times of hardship, and that it is important to be realistic and practical in one's approach to life

word_list.word: prosperity (makes|gains) friends, and adversity tries them
word_list.pronunciation: pɹəspˈɛɹɪɾi mˌeɪks ɡˈeɪnz fɹˈɛndz ænd ædvˈɚsɪɾi tɹˈaɪz ðˌɛm

senses.definition: used to imply that during prosperous times, people are more likely to form friendships, while during challenging times, the strength and authenticity of those friendships are tested

word_list.phonetic_text: sʌm.taɪm dɑ:rk.nɪs kæn ʃoʊ ju ðə laɪt

senses.definition: used to suggest that difficult or challenging experiences can sometimes lead to positive outcomes or insights, emphasizing the importance of resilience and perseverance in the face of adversity

word_list.word: the longest mile is the last mile home
word_list.pronunciation: ðə lˈɑːŋɡəst mˈaɪl ɪz ðə lˈæst mˈaɪl hˈoʊm

senses.definition: used to suggest that the final part of a journey or task often feels more difficult and takes more time than the earlier stages

word_list.word: there is no royal road to learning
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈoʊ ɹˈɔɪəl ɹˈoʊd tə lˈɜːnɪŋ

senses.definition: used to imply that there are no shortcuts or easy paths to acquiring knowledge or expertise, and that the only way to truly learn something is through hard work, study, and practice

word_list.word: when it rains, it pours
word_list.pronunciation: wˌɛn ɪt ɹˈeɪnz ɪt pˈoːɹz

senses.definition: used to suggest that when something bad happens, other bad things often happen in quick succession or in large numbers, making the situation seem even worse

senses.definition: used to imply that someone or something that is considered undesirable or unwelcome will inevitably reappear or resurface, even if they have been supposedly eliminated or dealt with

word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dʒˈɛm ɪz nˌɑːt pˈɑːlɪʃt wɪðˌaʊt ɹˈʌbɪŋ nˈɔːɹ ɐ mˈæn pɚfˈɛktᵻd wɪðˌaʊt tɹˈaɪəlz

senses.definition: used to suggest that difficult experiences and challenges are necessary for personal growth and development, and that one should embrace them as opportunities for self-improvement

word_list.word: above black there is no color (, and above salt there is no savor|)

word_list.word: calamity is man's true touchstone

senses.definition: used to imply that it is easy to appear virtuous and noble when everything is going well, but it is during times of hardship that a person's true nature is tested

word_list.word: crosses are ladders that lead to heaven

senses.definition: used to emphasize that everyone has their own struggles and pain that they carry within them, even if it is not outwardly visible or apparent to others

examples.x: Sally understood from Peter's words that even though outwardly he seemed happy, every heart has its own ache that others cannot always see.

word_list.word: every path has its (own|) puddle
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi pˈæθ hɐz ɪts ˈoʊn pˈʌdəl
